title: tanacetum sinuatum
intro: Tanacetum sinuatum, also known as the pyrethrum or feverfew, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Tanacetum sinuatum include reducing fever, relieving headaches and migraines, and alleviating menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to improve cognitive function and reduce anxiety. Tanacetum sinuatum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a dried flower in potpourri and crafts.
